page: Amazon Web Services r5a.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration provides 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core performance is low, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests placing in the bottom 10% of all tested systems. Conversely, multi-core performance is average, with multi-threaded Geekbench, stress-ng, and PassMark CPU tests landing in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests, including both cached and uncached read/write operations, also perform in the average tier.

This instance class offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high single-threaded compute performance. The high memory density relative to vCPUs makes it suitable for large-scale caching layers, in-memory databases, and moderate multi-threaded data processing. It is less suited for compute-bound tasks, single-threaded applications, or large language model inference, where benchmarks show weak or poor performance.
description: Amazon Web Services r5a.8xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: single-core metrics, including Geekbench and compression tasks, fall into the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks consistently rank in the middle 50%. This profile makes the r5a.8xlarge qualitatively cost-effective for memory-dense, multi-threaded workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and general-purpose backend applications that do not demand high single-threaded compute velocity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5a.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, offering average multi-core performance but low single-core speeds.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5a.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7571 processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring high DDR4 memory density and balanced multi-core performance for memory-intensive, non-compute-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
Equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz
Delivers average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Exhibits low single-core performance and lacks local storage
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded processing
